subject: PLACEHOLDER 1: Please commit to scrapping VNI West
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 1. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

I'm writing as a supporter of rural Australia to ask you, respectfully, to commit to scrapping the VNI West transmission project and the Western Renewables Link. These lines would cut across prime farmland and push families off properties they have worked for generations.

I know the party hasn't locked in its position yet, and that's exactly why I'm writing now. A firm commitment to protect this country would mean the world to the communities in its path.

Please land on the right side of this. Thank you for reading.

Kind regards,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 2: Stand with farmers on the Western Renewables Link
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 2. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

I am asking you, as someone who cares deeply about our farming communities, to oppose both VNI West and the Western Renewables Link. Good energy policy should not come at the cost of the families who feed the nation.

The people along these routes are not against progress. They are asking for a fair go and for their land and livelihoods to be respected.

Please consider committing the party to scrapping these projects. It would be the right and decent thing to do.

With respect and thanks,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 3: A polite request about VNI West
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 3. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

I'm reaching out about the VNI West and Western Renewables Link transmission projects. These would take prime farmland and disrupt communities across regional Victoria and New South Wales.

I understand the party is still forming its view. I'd like to gently urge you to commit to scrapping these projects and to back the farmers who steward this land.

Thank you for taking the time to hear from an ordinary supporter who believes you can do good here.

Sincerely,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 4: Protect prime farmland from these transmission lines
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 4. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

Our best farmland is finite, and once it's carved up for transmission corridors it does not come back. VNI West and the Western Renewables Link would do exactly that to families who have farmed for generations.

I'm writing to ask, respectfully, that the Liberal Party commit to scrapping both projects.

Rural Australia is watching this decision closely and hoping you'll stand with us. Thank you for your consideration.

Warm regards,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 5: Please hear rural Australia on VNI West
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 5. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

This is a heartfelt request from someone who wants the best for regional communities. VNI West and the Western Renewables Link threaten the farmland and the families that make rural Australia what it is.

The party still has time to do the right thing and commit to scrapping these projects. I hope you will.

Thank you for reading my email and for considering the people in the path of these lines.

Kind regards,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 6: Commit to scrapping the Western Renewables Link
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 6. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

I'm writing to respectfully ask that you oppose the Western Renewables Link and VNI West. These projects would push families off land they've worked for generations and split communities that deserve better.

A clear commitment from the Liberal Party to scrap them would send a powerful message that rural voices matter.

Please do the right thing. Thank you for your time and for genuinely considering this request.

Respectfully,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 7: Farmers are asking for your support
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 7. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

Farming families across VNI West and Western Renewables Link corridors are worried, and they're asking for help. These transmission projects would take prime agricultural land and change these communities forever.

I'm asking you, politely and sincerely, to commit the party to scrapping both projects.

You have the chance to stand up for the people who feed this nation. I hope you'll take it. Thank you.

With thanks,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 8: There's still time to do the right thing
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 8. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

The party hasn't locked in its position on VNI West and the Western Renewables Link yet, so I'm writing while it still counts. These projects would take prime farmland and uproot families with deep roots in their communities.

Please commit to scrapping them. It would mean a great deal to rural Australia.

Thank you for reading, and for weighing what this means for the families in the firing line.

Kind regards,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 9: A neighbour's request on transmission projects
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 9. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

I'm writing the way you'd write to a neighbour: honestly and with respect. VNI West and the Western Renewables Link would harm farmland and families across regional Australia.

I'd be grateful if the Liberal Party would commit to scrapping both projects and stand with the communities affected.

Thank you for hearing me out. I believe you can do the right thing here, and I hope you will.

Sincerely,

subject: PLACEHOLDER 10: Please protect our farming communities
body:
PLACEHOLDER COPY — variation 10. To be replaced with James's approved master email before launch.

Dear Liberal Party leadership,

I care about the future of rural Australia, and that's why I'm asking you to oppose VNI West and the Western Renewables Link. These projects would take prime farmland and displace families who have worked it for generations.

A commitment from the Liberal Party to scrap them would protect land, livelihoods and trust.

Please consider this request carefully. Thank you for your time and for the good you can do.

Warm regards,
